design_summary: Round 3 of the bounded candidate-language programme. Two justified families (a Tyrrhenian/Etruscan-related family and a pre-Greek Aegean-isolate substrate model) plus an agnostic random control, each a BOUNDED preregistered correspondence (phoneme inventory + allowed changes into the LA syllabary) + a SMALL cited lexicon of candidate-language WORDS. Each lexeme is expanded to its predicted LA syllabogram-value string(s) via the FIXED correspondence (never read off LA). A held-out LA multi-sign word MATCHES a family iff its value-string is a predicted whole-word form, or (for affix entries) it ends with a predicted affix string. Primary statistic = token-weighted match rate on the held-out multi-sign syllabic word set. Scored vs the WP6 multi-family end-to-end null: (S) order-shuffle, (W) wrong-language opaque LB length-matched, (R) random-prior. Decisive nulls = W and R. FWER per family = fraction of decisive null realizations with match >= real. Holm across families. Leave-one-lexeme-out. 300-draw random-lexicon false-clear calibration.
non_circularity: Correspondences map candidate-language PHONEMES to syllabogram VALUES; predicted forms are DERIVED from candidate-language dictionary words (Etruscan lexicon; Beekes' Pre-Greek reconstructions), not fitted to LA. LB values only ever appear as the wrong-language NULL corpus. The conventional LA transliteration is a fixed published convention applied identically to every corpus and family.
bounded_no_root_search: Lexicons are fixed a priori (6 entries/family), from cited proposals; no unbounded thesaurus search. The combinatorial expansion size (effective_n predicted forms) is reported and is automatically deflated because every null realization runs the SAME expansion/matcher.
syllabogram_decomposition_note: Each AB syllabogram is decomposed to (onset_consonant, vowel) by standard Ventris-Chadwick values in the runner. Pure-vowel signs have empty onset.

TYR_Etruscan.gloss: Tyrrhenian / Etruscan-related. Sources: Bonfante & Bonfante, The Etruscan Language (2002); Facchetti, the Lemnos-stele Tyrrhenian link and Minoan-as-Tyrrhenian proposals; de Simone. Etruscan has NO voiced stops and NO /o/ (four-vowel a/e/i/u system) -- both constraints encoded in the correspondence. Core lexemes (year, son, wife, magistrate) + genitive/dative suffixes derived through the fixed correspondence.

AEG_isolate_substrate.gloss: Pre-Greek Aegean-isolate substrate model (a non-Indo-European, non-Semitic isolate reconstructed ONLY from Greek loanwords/toponyms). Sources: Beekes, Pre-Greek: Phonology, Morphology, Lexicon (2014); Furnee. Deliberately speculative -- the honest test of whether a substrate isolate reconstructed from Greek predicts held-out LA forms. Characteristic -nth-/-ss- suffixes and substrate nouns derived through the fixed correspondence.

lexicon.phonemes: a s a m i n

CTRL_agnostic_random_r3.gloss: Agnostic negative control: a fixed random lexicon of matched size/length-structure, no linguistic motivation. Expected FWER ~ uniform; verifies the pipeline is not rigged to pass. If this control clears the raw bar, no round-3 family can COUNT.
CTRL_agnostic_random_r3.lexicon: GENERATED at runtime: 6 random entries (4 word + 2 suffix) with lengths matched to the round-3 real families, phonemes drawn C/V-alternating from this inventory under fixed seed 20260708.

held_out_target: GORILA word units, syllabic-only, multi-sign (>=2 signs): 973 tokens. Single-sign words excluded (non-discriminating for lexical hypotheses).
primary_statistic: token-weighted match rate = sum token-freq(matching words)/total tokens, on the multi-sign held-out set.
decision_rule: A family COUNTS iff Holm-adjusted decisive FWER < 0.05 AND leave-one-lexeme-out worst decisive FWER < 0.05 AND it beats the random-lexicon calibration (real percentile p<0.05, false-clear rate <=0.10) AND the agnostic control does NOT clear the raw bar.
prior_expectation: Rounds 1-2 were AT_END_TO_END_NULL. Honest expectation for round 3 is again AT_END_TO_END_NULL; run properly and report which family, if any, COUNTS.
